title: Meiogyne caudata
intro: Meiogyne caudata is a species of plant in the family Annonaceae, commonly known as a type of fruit-bearing tree. The health benefits of Meiogyne caudata include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include annonaceous acetogenins, which are known for their anti-cancer and neuroprotective activities. Meiogyne caudata is typically used to make herbal preparations such as tinctures and infusions, which are often used to treat fever, coughs, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Meiogyne caudata is classified under the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Annonaceae. This family consists of flowering plants commonly known as custard apples or soursop.
